notes: These are startup tasks only; no feature implementation is included.
Use uv as the Python package manager and workflow tool.

tasks.title: Initialize uv-based Python project scaffold
tasks.goal: Create the base project structure and packaging metadata for pytest-doctor.
instructions: Initialize project metadata with uv (pyproject.toml, package name pytest-doctor, Python >=3.8).
Set up src layout: src/pytest_doctor/ and tests/ directories.
Create package entry points and a minimal module skeleton for analyzers, scoring, reporter, and CLI namespaces.
Add core metadata (description, license placeholder, authors placeholder).
Generate lockfile via uv and document basic uv commands in README or CONTRIBUTING.
acceptance_criteria: `uv sync` succeeds on a clean checkout.
Project imports `pytest_doctor` without errors.
Directory layout matches docs architecture at a high level.

tasks.title: Set up developer tooling with uv
tasks.goal: Enable formatting, linting, typing, and test execution from day one.
instructions: Add dev dependencies using uv for pytest, coverage, ruff, and mypy.
Define tool configuration in pyproject.toml for linting, formatting, and type checking.
Create standard commands/scripts documented with uv usage (e.g., lint, test, typecheck).
Add a basic test runner configuration and coverage defaults aligned with docs expectations.
deliverables: Documented uv command workflow for contributors
acceptance_criteria: `uv run pytest` executes successfully (even with placeholder tests).
`uv run ruff check .` and `uv run mypy src` run without config errors.
Coverage command is runnable via uv.

tasks.title: Define core data contracts and result models
tasks.goal: Implement shared dataclasses/types that all analyzers and reporters will use.
instructions: Create initial models for Diagnostic, Gap, EdgeCase, Score, and Results based on architecture docs.
Define enums/constants for severity and category conventions.
Add serialization helpers for JSON output use cases.
Write unit tests validating model construction and schema stability.
deliverables: Minimal schema examples in docs or docstrings
acceptance_criteria: All core models can be imported and instantiated.
Model tests pass via uv-run pytest.
JSON serialization shape supports CLI/API requirements.

tasks.title: Create analysis pipeline skeleton and module boundaries
tasks.goal: Lay out non-functional analyzer interfaces and orchestration flow before full logic.
instructions: Create analyzer modules: code_analyzer, test_analyzer, coverage_engine, gap_detector, edge_case_detector, quality_analyzer.
Define interface signatures and placeholder return objects consistent with docs.
Add a correlation engine and top-level diagnose orchestration function skeleton.
Ensure clean import graph and explicit boundaries between analysis stages.
deliverables: Smoke tests for end-to-end control flow with placeholder data
acceptance_criteria: `diagnose()` executes through the full pipeline without unhandled exceptions.
Each stage is represented by a dedicated module/class as documented.
Smoke tests pass using uv-run pytest.

tasks.title: Implement CLI command surface skeleton
tasks.goal: Expose documented command options and output modes with placeholder behavior.
instructions: Create CLI entrypoint `pytest-doctor` wired through pyproject script configuration.
Add argument parsing for key flags from docs (verbose, score, format, diff, config, project).
Integrate CLI with diagnose() skeleton and reporter placeholders.
Return stable exit codes and helpful usage text.
deliverables: CLI module and registered console script
acceptance_criteria: `uv run pytest-doctor --help` works.
CLI accepts major flags without crashing.
Exit codes are deterministic for success/failure paths.

tasks.title: Add configuration loading and validation skeleton
tasks.goal: Support documented config files and defaults early in the project lifecycle.
instructions: Create config loader supporting pytest_doctor.config.json and pyproject.toml sources.
Implement merge strategy: defaults < file config < runtime args.
Add schema validation with clear error messages for invalid options.
Provide tests for precedence and validation behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Valid config files load successfully.
Invalid configs fail with actionable errors.
Precedence rules are covered by tests.

tasks.title: Set up CI baseline for uv workflows
tasks.goal: Create an automated quality gate to keep scaffolding stable as implementation begins.
instructions: Add CI workflow that installs uv, syncs dependencies, and runs lint/typecheck/tests.
Include coverage command execution and artifact/report output if feasible.
Ensure CI uses pinned Python versions compatible with project constraints.
Document local parity commands using uv.
deliverables: Contributor docs for running CI-equivalent commands locally
acceptance_criteria: CI passes on initial scaffold branch.
Local uv commands mirror CI steps.
Failures are easy to interpret from logs.

tasks.title: Prepare implementation backlog from documentation
tasks.goal: Turn design docs into an executable backlog for phased delivery.
instructions: Break down RULES, GAP_DETECTION, EDGE_CASES, and SCORING docs into milestone epics.
Define MVP scope (minimum useful diagnosis path) and post-MVP enhancements.
Estimate complexity and identify cross-team dependencies.
Produce a staged delivery sequence aligned to analyzer architecture.
acceptance_criteria: Backlog covers all major documented features.
MVP scope is explicit and time-boxable.
Dependencies and risks are called out clearly.
